Jae Woong Hwang Resume Experience Toolchains

Public-Safe Production ADAS Case Study

Commercial-Vehicle ADAS Controls & Validation

This page summarizes my public-safe experience in production commercial-vehicle ADAS controls and validation at Hyundai Motor Company. The work involved selected SCC/L2 ADAS behavior logic, calibration and validation workflows, production issue response, vehicle-level debugging, and practical toolchains across heavy-duty truck, tractor, bus, ICE, and FCEV platforms. Proprietary source code, signal names, calibration metadata, CAN database content, calibration values, thresholds, vehicle logs, and internal implementation details are intentionally omitted.

Back to resume
Role Commercial-Vehicle ADAS Controls & Validation Engineer
Platforms Truck, tractor, and bus variants with ICE and FCEV powertrains
Workflow MILS, HILS, dynamometer, real-vehicle validation, and log replay
Toolchain CANape, VN1640, XCP/CAN-FD, MF4, MATLAB/Simulink, Python/C++ validation tooling

Ownership Boundary

I did not own the entire SCC/L2 ADAS stack end-to-end. My work focused on selected production-facing behavior logic, validation workflows, calibration/debugging support, production issue analysis, and public-safe workflow toolchains within the broader ADAS development team. This page describes my individual contribution areas in public-safe form.

Role

Commercial-vehicle ADAS controls and validation engineer focused on production SCC/L2 ADAS behavior, target-selection robustness, road-curvature estimation, and validation workflow improvement.

Platforms

Worked across commercial vehicle variants including truck, tractor, and bus platforms with ICE and FCEV powertrains.

Responsibilities

  • Production-facing ADAS behavior logic development for selected SCC/L2 functions.
  • SCC emergency-stop state behavior development and validation.
  • Road-radius fusion logic development using steering-intent, yaw-rate, and conditional lane/camera validity concepts.
  • SCC/FOC tuning, state-transition updates, calibration, regression testing, and release-readiness support.
  • Target-selection robustness improvement and false-detection analysis using real logs and BEV replay workflows.
  • MILS/HILS/dynamometer/real-vehicle validation and production issue response.
  • Internal tooling and workflow improvement for validation bottleneck reduction and future AI-validation transition.

Independent Technical Ownership

Public-safe case-study cards focused on selected production-facing contribution areas.

SCC Emergency-Stop State Behavior

Problem

Production commercial-vehicle SCC/L2 workflows require deterministic and safe behavior when the driver is incapacitated or unable to respond.

My contribution
  • Independently developed and validated SCC emergency-stop behavior for selected production-facing commercial-vehicle ADAS workflows.
  • Structured the public description around safe handover and stop-state behavior without exposing internal state names, thresholds, or calibration values.
Outcome

Supported robust stop-state behavior for selected SCC/L2 ADAS workflows in commercial-vehicle production development.

Road-Radius Fusion Logic

Problem

Heavy-duty vehicles have long wheelbases and slower yaw response than passenger vehicles. During lane changes or curvature transitions, yaw-rate-only road-radius estimation can be delayed, while camera/lane information may be unreliable in commercial-vehicle conditions.

My contribution
  • Independently developed production-applied road-radius fusion logic using steering-wheel/tire-angle-based RoadRadius and EBS yaw-rate-based RoadRadius.
  • Developed lane-validity conditions so lane information is blended only when usable, because commercial-vehicle camera/lane information was not always reliable.
  • Built a steering-wheel-to-tire-angle conversion model and used a 2D bicycle-model concept to account for understeer and estimate steering-intent-based radius.
  • Fused steering-based and yaw-rate-based radius estimates through an Extended Kalman Filter for faster and more stable RoadRadius estimation.
Outcome

Improved ego-path prediction robustness under long-wheelbase dynamics, lane changes, curvature transitions, and unreliable lane-perception conditions.

Public-Safe Road-Radius Fusion Architecture

Steering input can represent driver intention earlier than yaw-rate response. The public-safe architecture is framed around steering-intent radius estimation, yaw-rate-based radius estimation, lane-validity checking, fusion, and conditional lane blending.

  • Steering input is converted into a vehicle-dynamics-based curvature estimate.
  • Vehicle speed and yaw rate provide a separate road-radius estimate.
  • Fusion logic combines estimates while checking lane/camera validity.
  • Final road-radius output supports ego-path prediction and target selection.

Front-Target-Based Brake Distribution Logic

Problem

When a forward target vehicle is present and the ADAS controller requests longitudinal deceleration, heavy-duty vehicles must satisfy the requested deceleration while avoiding unnecessary overuse of the foundation brake. This is especially important during long downhill driving, where foundation-brake overuse can create thermal, durability, fuel-efficiency, and driver-acceptance issues.

My contribution
  • Participated in SCC tuning and front-object-control-related tuning work within the SCC part.
  • Worked on rule-based brake distribution concepts that select auxiliary brake and foundation brake usage according to ADAS-requested deceleration and ego/forward-target conditions.
  • Used scenario factors such as ego speed, forward-target speed, speed gradient, relative distance, and required deceleration to decide brake contribution.
  • Balanced heavy-duty deceleration quality with brake durability, fuel efficiency, and driver acceptance.
Outcome

Improved front-target deceleration behavior robustness across commercial-vehicle scenarios where braking strategy matters as much as pure deceleration tracking.

Public-Safe Front-Target Brake Distribution Architecture

For heavy-duty vehicles, braking strategy during forward-target deceleration must consider not only requested deceleration but also thermal load, downhill duration, brake durability, fuel efficiency, and driver acceptance. The diagram keeps the logic rule-based and public-safe.

  • ADAS-requested deceleration is interpreted together with ego speed, forward-target speed, speed gradient, and relative distance.
  • Auxiliary brake is prioritized when the situation allows, especially in long downhill or high thermal-load scenarios.
  • Foundation brake is blended when required to satisfy deceleration demand and safety constraints.
  • The final brake command balances safety, durability, fuel efficiency, and driver acceptance.

Target-Selection Robustness and BEV Replay Validation

Problem

Curved-road, bus-only-lane, and large-road scenarios can create false or unstable target behavior risk when predicted ego path and actual road geometry diverge. Reproducing the same scenario repeatedly with a real bus can require long test trips and rare traffic conditions.

My contribution
  • Used a BEV target-selection validation studio to replay real logs through the same TOS/ODP logic used for production ECU behavior analysis.
  • Investigated why false target selection occurred, compared logic/parameter changes, and prepared fixes before final real-vehicle confirmation.
  • Applied this workflow across the commercial-vehicle programs listed on this page, with especially strong usefulness during bus development.
Outcome

Reduced repeated bus-only-lane road-test loops from about 10 trips to about 5 for similar false-detection issues. Since each trip could take roughly 4-5 hours, the tool helped cut scenario-debugging test time by about half before final real-vehicle confirmation.

Validation and Release Support

Problem

Selected control-logic areas must be validated across platform, regulation, powertrain, brake-system, and field-test constraints before release.

My contribution
  • Supported calibration, regression testing, vehicle-level debugging, and production release-readiness workflows across multiple commercial-vehicle platforms.
  • Participated in MILS, HILS, dynamometer, and real-vehicle validation workflows.
  • Analyzed production-development issues and supported logic/calibration updates under platform, regulation, and field-test constraints.
Outcome

Helped move selected ADAS behavior updates through production-development validation workflows.

Production Vehicle References

Mass-production vehicle programs where the commercial-vehicle ADAS logic was applied. Different powertrain, transmission, and brake-control configurations made behavior consistency and calibration substantially harder.

QZ FCEV Europe

XCIENT Fuel Cell Truck FCEV

FCEV / AT / EBS

Public reference platform where commercial-vehicle SCC/L2 ADAS behavior was applied and validated for production-development workflows.

QZ FCEV North America

XCIENT Fuel Cell Tractor FCEV

FCEV / AT / ABS

Public reference platform requiring ADAS behavior consistency under a different brake-control configuration.

QZ 25PE ICE

The new XCIENT ICE Tractor

ICE / AMT / EBS

Public reference platform where transmission and brake-control differences made calibration and behavior consistency non-trivial.

PY ICE / FCEV Bus

Commercial bus platform

ICE/FCEV / AT / EBS

Bus platform where target-selection robustness, ego-path prediction, and real-road validation scenarios were especially important.

Production Validation Workflow

  • Model-based development and logic review in MATLAB/Simulink-oriented production workflow.
  • Vehicle-level SCC/L2 calibration and validation across heavy-duty platform variants.
  • Scenario-focused debugging for false target selection, curvature transitions, braking behavior, and lane-perception reliability.
  • Production issue response and release-readiness support with test teams.

Public-Safe Toolchain Connection

Separate portfolio toolchain pages show public-safe versions of engineering tools built to reduce validation bottlenecks and prepare for AI-based ADAS validation. The first two toolchains are proactive AI-transition platforms built under commercial-vehicle resource constraints; they are not presented as already deployed team-wide production tools. The BEV target-selection validation studio reflects a workflow that was actively used during commercial-vehicle production development to investigate real-log false-detection behavior.

  • Legacy Calibration Setup to ADAS AI Validation Loop: resource-constrained prototype using CANape/VN1640/laptop field workflow to help module owners experiment with learned models on real vehicle signals.
  • ADAS Log-to-AI Workbench: MF4-to-AI transition workflow for converting familiar measurement logs into reusable datasets, model training runs, and exportable artifacts.
  • BEV Target Selection Validation Studio: production-development replay workflow using real logs and TOS/ODP logic to reduce repeated road-test loops for false target-selection analysis.

Patents & Inventions

Inventor / co-inventor on approximately seven ADAS and vehicle-control-related patent filings, including Korean patent filings and selected US filings in progress. Details for unpublished or pending filings remain omitted.

Public References
